quote.text: In every walk with nature, one receives far more than he seeks.
quote.author: John Muir

fun_fact.text: Hummingbirds are the only birds that can fly backwards! They can also hover in mid-air by flapping their wings in a figure-eight pattern up to 80 times per second. The Olympic Peninsula is home to both Anna's and Rufous hummingbirds during summer months.

short_read.title: The Secret Lives of Hummingbirds
short_read.teaser: These tiny jewels of the bird world have hearts that beat over 1,200 times per minute and memories that help them remember every flower they've visited. Discover the amazing abilities of these backyard visitors.

medium_read.title: The Golden Age of Hungarian Thermal Baths
medium_read.teaser: Hungary has more thermal water springs than almost any other country, and bathing culture has been central to Hungarian life for over 2,000 years. From Roman times to the Ottoman era to today, these healing waters tell the story of a nation.

long_read.title: Olympic National Park: A Crown Jewel of Biodiversity
long_read.teaser: From temperate rainforests to alpine peaks to wild Pacific coastline, the Olympic Peninsula contains more ecological diversity than almost anywhere on Earth. This is the story of how this unique landscape was preserved for future generations.

port_angeles.title: Harbor Seal Pupping Season
port_angeles.description: July is peak pupping season for harbor seals along the Strait of Juan de Fuca! Mother seals often rest on beaches and rocks with their pups, teaching them to swim and hunt. The best viewing spots include Ediz Hook and Dungeness Spit, but remember to keep at least 100 yards away to avoid disturbing these new families.

puzzle_1.question: A hummingbird's heart beats about ___ times per minute during flight.
puzzle_1.correct_response: That's right! Can you imagine — 1,200 beats per minute! Your heart is probably beating around 70 times per minute right now. These tiny birds are truly remarkable.
puzzle_1.close_response: You're very close! The answer is 1,200 beats per minute. That's about 20 times faster than a human heart at rest!
puzzle_1.wrong_response: The answer is 1,200 beats per minute! It's hard to imagine a heart beating that fast, but hummingbirds need all that energy to power their incredible flying abilities.
puzzle_1.learn_more_text: To support their super-fast metabolism, hummingbirds must eat about half their body weight in sugar every day and visit hundreds of flowers. At night, they enter a hibernation-like state called torpor to conserve energy.

puzzle_2.question: Which Hungarian word means 'butterfly'?
puzzle_2.correct_response: Beautiful! Pillangó is such a lovely word for these delicate creatures. The word comes from the verb 'pillanni' which means 'to blink' — perhaps because butterflies move so quickly!
puzzle_2.wrong_response: The answer is pillangó! It's such a musical word. Madár means bird, virág means flower, and csillag means star — all beautiful words too!
puzzle_2.learn_more_text: Hungary is home to about 160 species of butterflies, including the beautiful Scarce Swallowtail. Many Hungarian folk songs and poems feature pillangók as symbols of transformation and summer.

puzzle_3.question: What unique feature makes the Olympic Peninsula's rainforests special?
options: They're the driest rainforests on Earth
They're the only temperate rainforests in North America
They have no trees over 50 feet tall
puzzle_3.correct_response: Exactly right! The Olympic Peninsula's temperate rainforests are incredibly rare — they're among only a handful of such ecosystems in the entire world. You're living next to something truly special!
puzzle_3.wrong_response: The answer is that they're the only temperate rainforests in North America! Unlike tropical rainforests, these forests thrive in cool, moist conditions and feature different species like Sitka spruce and western hemlock.
puzzle_3.learn_more_text: The Hoh Rainforest receives 12-14 feet of rain per year, creating a magical landscape of moss-draped trees, ferns, and enormous old-growth trees. Some trees in these forests are over 500 years old.

game.title: Bubble Shooter
game.description: A relaxing bubble puzzle game — aim and pop matching colored bubbles. Easy to pick up, fun to play!
